2020-08-20.abstract: An apprentice cook, claiming he wanted to die after an affair went awry, was apprehended by the police after forcing the passenger jet to land. No one was injured.

2020-08-23.abstract: Instead of mass-casualty attacks, the Taliban are carrying out targeted assaults in the capital and neighboring districts. The country’s security forces seem unable to control them.

2020-08-24.abstract: For 26 years, President Aleksandr Lukashenko has built a regime in his own eccentric, authoritarian image. Now he faces the greatest challenge yet to his hold on power.

2020-08-24.title: At Least 14 Killed After Suicide Bombers Hit Philippines
2020-08-24.abstract: Soldiers and civilians were among the dead. The explosions occurred in a town plaza and near a cathedral targeted in a suicide bombing last year.

2020-08-24.title: A Lawyer’s Tweets Put India’s Supreme Court on Trial and Him at Risk of Imprisonment
2020-08-24.abstract: Prashant Bhushan, a prominent lawyer who criticized India’s Supreme Court, could be sentenced to jail after he was found in contempt.

2020-08-24.title: Florida Judge Strikes Down Order Requiring Schools to Physically Reopen Amid Covid-19 Risks
2020-08-24.abstract: Quarantine rules are expanding around the world, but not in America. The virus is circulating in Gaza. Researchers in Hong Kong find that in rare cases, a person can get reinfected.

2020-08-24.title: On Mideast Trip, Pompeo Mixes Diplomacy With Partisan Politics
2020-08-24.abstract: The secretary of state’s five-day tour is intended to discuss regional peace and security. He also planned to use Jerusalem as a backdrop for an address to the Republican National Convention.

2020-08-25.title: For Refusing a Drink With the Boss, He Was Slapped in the Face
2020-08-25.abstract: A Chinese bank apologized for the “drunken misconduct” of two directors at a work dinner. The episode led to scrutiny of the country’s professional drinking culture.

2020-08-25.title: German Town Fears Ruin by U.S. Effort to Stop Russian Pipeline
2020-08-25.abstract: Three U.S. senators and the Trump administration want to halt Gazprom’s Nord Stream 2 pipeline by imposing sanctions on a Baltic port that is supplying the project.

2020-08-25.title: C.D.C. Changes Testing Guidance to Exclude People Without Covid-19 Symptoms, Worrying Experts
2020-08-25.abstract: The U.S. first lady, Melania Trump, called Covid-19 an “invisible enemy” and acknowledged the pandemic’s human toll. Kim Jong-un acknowledged ‘shortcomings’ in North Korea’s fight against the virus.

2020-08-25.title: ‘GolfGate’ Dinner in Ireland Sparks Political Backlash
2020-08-25.abstract: Two senior politicians have resigned, and the European Union’s trade commissioner is under pressure to quit after flouting coronavirus restrictions at a large private dinner.

2020-08-25.title: Facebook Plans Legal Action After Thailand Tells It to Mute Critics
2020-08-25.abstract: After a warning from the government, the company restricted access to a Facebook group critical of the Thai monarchy. But it said it would challenge the order.

2020-08-26.title: Hong Kong Arrests Lawmakers and Bars Another Journalist
2020-08-26.abstract: As a crackdown on dissent continues, officials are trying to frame it as a necessary remedy for an increasingly ungovernable city.

2020-08-26.title: The Suffragists Fought to Redefine Femininity. The Debate Isn’t Over.
2020-08-26.abstract: The campaign for the right to vote crystallized notions of femininity and motherhood that women are still expected to navigate.

2020-08-26.title: Schools Can Reopen, Germany Finds, but Expect a ‘Roller Coaster’
2020-08-26.abstract: With nations determined to return to in-person learning, many will have trouble matching Germany’s formula: fast and free testing, robust contact tracing and low community spread.

2020-08-26.title: Almost 500 Million Children Are Cut Off From School in Pandemic, Report Says
2020-08-26.abstract: The Justice Department is considering an investigation of nursing-home outbreaks in four Democratic-led states, including New York. California makes a deal to more than double testing capacity.

2020-08-26.title: Nearly 80 Killed as Flash Floods Ravage City in Afghanistan
2020-08-26.abstract: The toll in Charikar was expected to rise as rescue workers sift through the wreckage of dozens of destroyed homes.

2020-08-27.title: New Zealand Gives Christchurch Killer a Record Sentence
2020-08-27.abstract: While the grieving and wounded watched, Brenton Tarrant was hauled away to face the certainty of dying behind bars after killing 51 people at two mosques in the worst terrorist attack New Zealand has ever seen.

2020-08-27.title: Desperate to Leave Beirut, Young Lebanese Are Also the Ones Fixing It
2020-08-27.abstract: The young people leading the huge volunteer effort after the recent blast are bringing hope, but have lost it themselves. “I want to at least have Beirut on its feet before I go,” one said.

2020-08-27.title: White House, Under Fire for Discouraging Coronavirus Testing, Says It Will Buy Millions of Test Kits
2020-08-27.abstract: President Trump promised a vaccine by the end of the year. For hurricane-battered Louisiana and Texas, a confluence of disaster.

2020-08-27.title: China Fires Missiles Into South China Sea, Sending U.S. a Message
2020-08-27.abstract: The move was Beijing’s latest effort to assert sovereignty over disputed waters.

2020-08-27.title: Mali’s Ousted President Is Released From Detention by Coup Leaders
2020-08-27.abstract: Ibrahim Boubacar Keïta was allowed to return to his home in the capital by the military officers who forced him to resign as president.

2020-08-28.title: Italy Foreign Minister Shares Blackface Images of His Summer Tan
2020-08-28.abstract: After Luigi Di Maio returned from vacation with a deep tan, images depicting him in a form of blackface filled Italy’s web. He shared pictures of himself as Michael Jordan and a Huxtable.

2020-08-28.title: A Doctor Asks: Is Covid Scaring Us Away From Our Humanity?
2020-08-28.abstract: The biggest threat posed by the coronavirus may not be its virulence, but rather the way it slowly erodes what it means to be human.

2020-08-28.title: Shinzo Abe, Japan’s Longest-Serving Prime Minister, Resigns Because of Illness
2020-08-28.abstract: Mr. Abe has been prime minister for nearly eight consecutive years, a significant feat in a country accustomed to high turnover in the top job.

2020-08-28.title: Unsealed Archives Give Fresh Clues to Pope Pius XII’s Response to the Holocaust
2020-08-28.abstract: Several documents that have emerged from the newly opened Vatican Archives on the papacy of Pius XII bolster accusations of indifference to Jewish suffering, but some scholars say the full picture has yet to emerge.

2020-08-28.title: California Moves Toward Easing Coronavirus Restrictions on Business
2020-08-28.abstract: Local health departments called on the C.D.C. to change new recommendations that people without symptoms do not need to be tested. Many infected children show no symptoms, a study found.

2020-08-29.title: Shinzo Abe’s Resignation Prompts Speculation About His Successor
2020-08-29.abstract: Whoever the Liberal Democratic Party elects as its leader, probably within the next week, will almost certainly become Japan’s next prime minister. It isn’t clear who that will be.

2020-08-29.title: European Leaders Weigh New Lockdowns as Cases Rise
2020-08-29.abstract: Some in Europe fear a return to lockdowns as cases rise. Colleges struggle to rein in sororities and fraternities.

2020-08-29.title: Berlin Police Halt March Against Coronavirus Restrictions, Saying Protesters Risked Spreading Disease
2020-08-29.abstract: Thousands had gathered in the German capital. But the march was shut down after an hour for failing to obey social distancing rules.

2020-08-29.title: Many Want to Be Japan’s New Leader. Do They Know What Awaits Them?
2020-08-29.abstract: The coronavirus, a tanking economy, an aggressive China, a postponed Olympics, a U.S. election: That’s just the start. And any successor will confront those challenges without having the stature of Shinzo Abe.

2020-08-29.title: Humans Take a Step Closer to ‘Flying Cars’
2020-08-29.abstract: A Japanese company said it had completed a manned flight of its electrical vertical takeoff and landing machine. Experts say the technology needs work and that it will be expensive.

2020-08-30.title: With a Wary Eye on China, Taiwan Moves to Revamp Its Military
2020-08-30.abstract: The self-governing democracy is taking steps to bolster its armed forces in order to deter or defeat an invasion from China — with or without American help.

2020-08-30.title: ‘A Hail Mary’: Psychedelic Therapy Draws Veterans to Jungle Retreats
2020-08-30.abstract: Ayahuasca, a vomit-inducing hallucinogenic brew, draws thousands of people each year — including former soldiers — to jungle retreats that have become an unlicensed and unregulated mental health marketplace.

2020-08-30.title: U.S. Coronavirus Cases Top 6 Million
2020-08-30.abstract: The jump in infections comes as the daily rate continues to decline. New Delhi is reopening its subway, even as India sets global records for new cases.

2020-08-30.title: Jeanette Carlson, Fighter Against Apartheid, Dies at 91
2020-08-30.abstract: She was deported from South Africa in 1971 for her activity and vowed never to return. “It was disgusting to live in that kind of environment,” she said.

2020-08-30.title: Taliban Violated Afghan Deal With Shelling of American Bases, U.S. Officials Say
2020-08-30.abstract: Rockets that U.S. officials say were fired by the insurgent group have landed around two bases used by American forces. A Taliban commander denied involvement.

2020-08-31.title: Eye on Beirut, Senegal Port Rushes to Truck Away Tons of Ammonium Nitrate
2020-08-31.abstract: After nearly 3,000 tons of ammonium nitrate caused a devastating explosion in Beirut, Lebanon, the West African nation of Senegal discovered the same amount sitting in its port in Dakar.

2020-08-31.title: Girl in Taiwan Is Swept High by a Kite
2020-08-31.abstract: Powerful winds swept a 3-year-old girl into the sky after she became entangled in the kite’s tail during a festival in Taiwan. The girl landed safely.

2020-08-31.title: Indian Court Hands Down Symbolic Sentence for Outspoken Lawyer
2020-08-31.abstract: The case against Prashant Bhushan, a public interest lawyer who criticized the Supreme Court and its chief justice in tweets, was seen as a test of free expression in the world’s largest democracy.

2020-08-31.title: The Midwest Sees a Spike as Covid-19 Cases Decline Elsewhere
2020-08-31.abstract: In nine U.S. states, case numbers are still rising or setting records. The virus is pummeling the economies of India and South Korea. Hong Kong began a Beijing-backed testing program.

2020-08-31.title: Lebanon Taps a New Prime Minister Amid Swirling Crises
2020-08-31.abstract: The country’s president has designated a diplomat, Mustafa Adib, to form a government that must address an economic meltdown and the aftermath of the Beirut explosion.
